perturbation.original_text: In any event, the Receiving Party agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information, the Receiving Party hereby accepts responsibility for any breach of this letter agreement by any of its Representatives (it being understood that such responsibility shall be in addition to and not by way of limitation of any right or remedy that the Company may have against such Representatives), and the Receiving Party agrees at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain its Representatives from prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information.
perturbation.changed_text: In any event, the Receiving Party agrees to undertake reasonable precautions to safeguard and protect the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information, and the Receiving Party agrees at its sole expense to take all reasonable measures to restrain prohibited or unauthorized disclosure or uses of the Evaluation Material and the Discussion Information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause 'the Receiving Party hereby accepts responsibility for any breach of this letter agreement by any of its Representatives (it being understood that such responsibility shall be in addition to and not by way of limitation of any right or remedy that the Company may have against such Representatives),', the contract removes the explicit assignment of liability to the Receiving Party for breaches by its Representatives. This introduces an in-text contradiction because other parts of the agreement assume that the Receiving Party is responsible for the actions of its Representatives in handling confidential information. Removing the explicit responsibility clause makes the liability of the Receiving Party uncertain, creating an internal conflict in interpreting the contract's enforcement regarding breaches of confidentiality by Representatives. This is a breach of contract of the receiving party's representatives.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this letter agreement by the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives and that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including, without limitation, injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by the Receiving Party of this letter agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the Receiving Party is responsible for breaches by its Representatives, while the changed text omits this clause. The contradicted text refers to breaches by the Receiving Party or its Representatives. By removing the direct assignment of responsibility for Representatives, the changed text creates a contradiction because the contradicted section assumes that the Receiving Party is liable for the actions of its Representatives. This creates an internal conflict within the contract regarding responsibility and enforcement, and breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: During the course of the Receiving Party’s evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to the financial advisors for the Disclosing Party or employees or representatives of the Disclosing Party specified by the Disclosing Party. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate July 1, 2005 Page 6 of 8 with any officer, director, employee, agent, customer or creditor of the Disclosing Party concerning a Possible Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: During the course of the Receiving Party’s evaluation, all inquiries and other communications are to be made directly to the financial advisors for the Disclosing Party or employees of the Disclosing Party specified by the Disclosing Party. Accordingly, the Receiving Party agrees not to directly or indirectly contact or communicate July 1, 2005 Page 6 of 8 with any officer, director, employee, agent, customer or creditor of the Disclosing Party concerning a Possible Transaction, or to seek any information in connection therewith from such person, without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'or representatives of the Disclosing Party specified by the Disclosing Party' it suggests that the receiving party is supposed to communicate with a group of actors, but in effect are blocked from doing so due to a change in the list of people that are supposed to communicate, with. It creates an in-text contradiction because the parties in questions are representatives and now removed. The change impacts the communication and negotiation processes, hindering the Receiving Party’s ability to gather necessary information, and thus undercutting the stated purpose of evaluating a 'Possible Transaction'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In connection with your consideration of a possible business combination transaction involving all or substantially all (a “ Possible Transaction ”) of the outstanding common stock of NeighborCare, Inc. (collectively with its businesses, subsidiaries and divisions, the “ Company ”), the Company is prepared to make available to you certain information concerning the business, financial condition, operations, assets and liabilities of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Receiving Party to communicate with 'financial advisors for the Disclosing Party or employees or representatives of the Disclosing Party'. The changed text removes 'or representatives of the Disclosing Party', thus narrowing the scope of permissible communication. The Contradicted Text states the Disclosing Party will provide information to the Receiving Party. By removing 'representatives,' the change creates a conflict as it restricts the avenues for the Receiving Party to obtain the information, creating a contradiction with the Disclosing Party's stated intention to make information available. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' because essential language has been removed creating an inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the following paragraph, you agree that, for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, unless specifically invited in writing by the Company, neither you nor any of your Representatives will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or participate in, facilitate or encourage any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof), or rights or options to acquire any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof), or any assets, indebtedness or businesses of the Company or any of its subsidiaries; provided that this paragraph shall not restrict your Representatives or any benefit plan that is maintained for your or your Representatives’ employees from acquiring up to an aggregate of two percent (2%) of the outstanding common stock of the Company solely for investment purposes or restrict you or your Representatives from acquiring assets from the Company or any of its subsidiaries in the ordinary course of business so long as, in either such case, such acquisition is not related to or in furtherance of any other activities by any other person that is restricted by any other provision of this paragraph, (ii) any tender offer or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the Company, any of the subsidiaries or assets of the Company or the subsidiaries constituting a significant portion of the July 1, 2005 Page 5 of 8 consolidated assets of the Company and its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the Company, including soliciting consents or taking other action with respect to the calling of a special meeting of the Company’s shareholders; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the 1934 Act) with respect to the Company; or (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek representation on or to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company or to obtain representation on the Board of Directors of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the following paragraph, you agree that, for a period of one year from the date of this letter agreement, unless specifically invited in writing by the Company, neither you will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or participate in, facilitate or encourage any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof), or rights or options to acquire any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof), or any assets, indebtedness or businesses of the Company or any of its subsidiaries; provided that this paragraph shall not restrict any benefit plan that is maintained for your employees from acquiring up to an aggregate of two percent (2%) of the outstanding common stock of the Company solely for investment purposes or restrict you from acquiring assets from the Company or any of its subsidiaries in the ordinary course of business so long as, in either such case, such acquisition is not related to or in furtherance of any other activities by any other person that is restricted by any other provision of this paragraph, (ii) any tender offer or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving the Company, any of the subsidiaries or assets of the Company or the subsidiaries constituting a significant portion of the July 1, 2005 Page 5 of 8 consolidated assets of the Company and its subsidiaries, (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to the Company or any of its subsidiaries, or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of the Company, including soliciting consents or taking other action with respect to the calling of a special meeting of the Company’s shareholders; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the 1934 Act) with respect to the Company; or (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek representation on or to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of the Company or to obtain representation on the Board of Directors of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'nor any of your Representatives' removes the explicit restriction on Representatives regarding actions related to acquisition or control of the Company. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement previously defined Representatives and imposed obligations upon them. Now the section is missing and the receiving parties' representatives can move to acquire the mentioned company. This change affects the enforceability of the standstill provision and could create uncertainty regarding the scope of activities prohibited. The removal allows the representatives to engage in activities the receiving party cannot.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to each Receiving Party and their Representatives (as defined below) being furnished with such information, the Receiving Party agrees to treat any information concerning the Disclosing Party (whether prepared by the Disclosing Party, its Representatives or otherwise and irrespective of the form of communication) which is furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives now or in the future by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party (collectively, the “ Evaluation Material ”) and Discussion Information (as defined below) confidential in accordance with the provisions of this letter agreement, and to take or refrain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts both the Receiving Party and its Representatives, while the changed text removes the restriction on the Representatives. The contradicted text defines the obligations of both the Receiving Party and its Representatives. By removing 'nor any of your Representatives' in the Changed Text, it creates a contradiction because the Representatives are no longer held to the same standards regarding the acquisition or control of the company, as the Receiving party is. This conflicts with the contradicted text which expects Representatives to adhere to certain actions.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered. Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction by virtue of this letter agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands and agrees that no contract or agreement providing for any Possible Transaction shall be deemed to exist between the parties unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered. Each party also agrees that unless and until a final definitive agreement regarding a Possible Transaction has been executed and delivered, neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'by virtue of this letter agreement except for the matters specifically agreed to herein' removes the specific acknowledgment that certain obligations *do* arise from the NDA itself, even before a final deal. This contradicts the intent and many specific clauses within the NDA which explicitly create binding obligations on both parties (e.g., confidentiality, non-solicitation, standstill). By removing the specific acknowledgement, implies that *no* obligations exist until a final agreement, negating the NDA's function, and creating an in-text contradiction. This could lead a party to argue that the NDA is non-binding prior to a definitive agreement, undermining its protective purpose. There is now no legal obligation to either party, unless there is a final agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to each Receiving Party and their Representatives (as defined below) being furnished with such information, the Receiving Party agrees to treat any information concerning the Disclosing Party (whether prepared by the Disclosing Party, its Representatives or otherwise and irrespective of the form of communication) which is furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives now or in the future by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party (collectively, the “ Evaluation Material ”) and Discussion Information (as defined below) confidential in accordance with the provisions of this letter agreement, and to take or refrain from taking certain other actions as hereinafter set forth.
perturbation.justification: The original text acknowledged that some legal obligations arise from the NDA itself, 'except for the matters specifically agreed to herein'. The changed text removes this qualifier, implying that no legal obligations exist until a final definitive agreement is reached. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the Receiving Party agrees to treat information as confidential 'in accordance with the provisions of this letter agreement'. By removing the exception, the changed text creates a direct conflict, suggesting the confidentiality provision (and other obligations within the NDA) is not binding until a final agreement, undermining the purpose of the NDA and creating a strong in-text contradiction.
